label: LifeOS Consumer Auth + Account System
point_b: LifeOS Consumer Alpha — user can sign up, log in, access their tier (Free/Core/Pro/Elite), and interact with Lumin as their account, not a guest
operator_lock: Adam 2026-06-29 — LifeOS needs user auth before consumer launch. Build per blueprint, run machine alpha, move on.

steps.label: User registration endpoint
steps.description: POST /api/v1/lifeos/auth/register — email+password, creates lifeos_users row, returns JWT. Validate with Zod. Hash password with bcrypt.

steps.label: Login + JWT issue
steps.description: POST /api/v1/lifeos/auth/login — verify password, issue access + refresh JWT pair (15m / 7d). Store refresh token hash in DB.

steps.label: DB migration — lifeos_users table
steps.description: CREATE TABLE lifeos_users: id, email (unique), password_hash, handle, tier (free/core/pro/elite), role, created_at, updated_at. Also lifeos_refresh_tokens table.

steps.label: Tier enforcement middleware
steps.description: requireLifeosTier(minTier) — reads JWT.tier, rejects if below minimum. Wire to premium endpoints.

steps.label: Login UI in lifeos-app.html
steps.description: Login/register drawer in the LifeOS app overlay. On success: store JWT in memory, update UI to show handle + tier badge. No localStorage for JWT.

steps.label: Lumin chat identifies account
steps.description: When user is logged in, Lumin knows their name, tier, and context. Chair route includes account JWT sub as user_id.

steps.label: Acceptance test + machine alpha
steps.description: npm run lifeos:user-auth:v1-acceptance — register, login, JWT verify, tier gate test, Lumin chat with account identity. Then run machine alpha walkthrough. All PASS → mark blueprint complete.
